ops.title: Subagent async wake is a harness gap, not a cc-interact/library capability
ops.body:
Investigation 2026-07-18/19 (5 agents: 3 source traces of cc-interact/cc-orchestrate/cc-review + authoritative claude-code-guide + upstream issue fetch), triggered by "should the codex skill be built on cc-interact" after the frozen-lanes incident.

**Definitive finding — an in-process subagent (Task/Agent tool, same OS process as parent) has no async interrupt inlet.** It advances only on a synchronous tool-call return or an inbound `SendMessage`. Corroborated by 9 independent open issues: #78782 (ours), #77578, #76594, #76681, #75043, #68749, #77300, #78434, #20754; framed as a feature request in #76203 ("a subagent has no interrupt inlet. Nothing outside can rouse it between turns"). No maintainer response, no roadmap.

- **Monitor tool**: loads and arms inside a subagent, but its wake is *dropped*, not queued (#77300 timestamped repro: teammate idles, event lands, 95s silence). Top-level Monitor DOES re-invoke the main session — the asymmetry is subagents/teammates only.
- **<channel> tags / MCP channel notifications**: target "the session"; subagents don't inherit --channels scope. No delivery.
- **SendMessage from parent/sibling**: the ONE working subagent-wake lever ("a completed subagent that receives a SendMessage auto-resumes"). Even it drops any pending Monitor event from the wake bundle.

**Why cc-interact can't help:** wake is a harness-scheduler capability, never a library one. cc-interact's own async (channel + Monitor over an SSE plane) is top-level-window-only for exactly this reason; cc-orchestrate gets async fan-out only because its "agents" are separate claude OS processes (tmux windows), not in-process subagents. So "build codex on cc-interact" is a category error for the subagent case, and v3's foreground-block is the convergent-correct pattern (cc-review reached it independently — CHANGELOG shows them abandoning resident-watcher-subagents for one-shot-blocking + relay-via-completion).

**Buildable constructions (no upstream dep):** (1) top-level "fire codex, keep working, get pinged" via `codex-ask watch` + native Monitor; (2) subagent async wake via a SendMessage-relay: a foreground-blocking relay agent loops `codex-ask watch --once` then SendMessage(owner); owner subagent dispatches non-blocking, yields, resumes on the nudge and reads its reply from disk. Sound but fragile (relay death -> owner deadlock until a top-level --ps sweep). Real fix for the general case is upstream (#78782 + siblings).

Related: v3 shipped as codex plugin 0.20.0 (commit ea751a9a). Decision to build both constructions + push upstream, not adopt cc-interact, taken 2026-07-19.
